subject: Mathematics
title: The Collatz Conjecture
description: A micro-lesson that makes The Collatz Conjecture usable.

hook.text: Pick any positive number. If even, halve it. If odd, triple it and add 1. Repeat.

buildup.text: No matter what number you start with, the sequence always seems to reach 1.

twist.text: Every starting number tested reaches 1. But no one can prove it always will.

climax.text: Paul Erdos said mathematics is not yet ready for such problems. He offered a cash prize.

punchline.text: A child can understand it. No one can solve it.

quiz.question: What happens in the Collatz sequence when a number is odd?
